characters/Jasper-Lee-Wilkins.title: Jasper Lee Wilkins
characters/Jasper-Lee-Wilkins.content:
Jasper Lee Wilkins



Appearance

Late 50s but wears it well — lean, sun-weathered, with deep laugh lines and a salt-and-pepper goatee that he keeps immaculately groomed despite everything else about him being slightly disheveled. Dresses in layered leathers and silks that were clearly expensive about ten years ago. Always has at least three visible knives and one hidden one. Moves like a man half his age when he needs to, but defaults to a lazy, rolling gait. His hands are never still — shuffling a coin, twirling a lockpick, gesturing while he talks.

Who Is He?
Jasper has been a fixture of the mercenary and adventuring scene for over thirty years. He’s been a hired sword, a scout, a con artist, a treasure hunter, a spy, and briefly — very briefly — a pirate. He’s competent at all of them and master of none, which is exactly how he likes it.
He has a reputation as someone who always delivers on a contract but might skim a little off the top. Someone who’ll watch your back in a fight but might forget to mention the ambush he knew about. Someone who’ll buy you a drink and pick your pocket while telling the funniest story you’ve ever heard.
Everyone who’s worked with Jasper has a Jasper story. Most of them end with “…and then the bastard disappeared before we split the gold.”

factions/Echoridge-Knights.title: Echoridge Knights
factions/Echoridge-Knights.content:
Echoridge Knights
What People Know
The Echoridge Knights are an order of dragon riders based in Echoridge. They are renowned across the fragments as elite mercenaries, guardians, and occasionally conquerors. Their services are available to anyone who can afford them — and reach the Perch to negotiate.
Reputation

Elite. A single Echoridge Knight and their dragon can turn the tide of a battle. A squad of them can end a war.
Unreachable. Echoridge is only accessible by flight. You don’t visit the Echoridge Knights. You send word and hope they answer.

Leadership
Echoridge is ruled by Lazziar Venfiel, known as the Umbraslayer — said to have slain the great wyrm Kais during the Fracturing. Lazziar is the only monarch Echoridge has ever known.

factions/The-Arkanis.title: The Arkanis
factions/The-Arkanis.content:
The Arkanis
What People Know
The Arkanis are a rapidly expanding military force that has been sweeping across the continent over the last several years. They conquer settlements — sometimes by force, sometimes by coercion — and install their own governors. Resistance is met with severe, public punishment.
They claim to be restoring the “original order” of Kainos — reuniting a fractured world under a single authority. Their soldiers call their conquests “liberations.”
Reputation

Disciplined. The Arkanis are not a horde or a rabble. They are organized, efficient, and well-supplied.
Dangerous. Their elite soldiers — the Heartsworn — wield magic that most militaries can’t match. Rumors say even common officers can cast spells.
Expanding. Villages and towns on the outskirts have been falling steadily. The major cities haven’t been touched yet, but the pattern is clear — the Arkanis are working their way inward.

What’s Less Known

Where they came from. The Arkanis seemed to appear out of nowhere within the last decade.
Who leads them. There are references to an Archon, but few have seen them.
Where their magic comes from. The Heartsworn’s abilities don’t look like traditional wizardry or divine blessing. Something else is going on.

Structure
What’s been observed from occupied territories:





























RankRoleArchonSupreme leader. Rarely seen.ProximarsRegional commandersCastellarsTown/city-level governors of occupied territoriesHeartswornElite soldiers with magical abilitiesSoldiersStandard troops, including conscripted locals

index.title: Kainos
index.content:
Welcome to Kainos

The world compendium for our campaign. Browse the wiki using the sidebar, search, or follow the links between pages.
Getting Started
New to the campaign? Start here:

Getting Started with Kainos
Your Story Hook
New Character Questionnaire

Explore the World

Astran · Deros · Tethermoor — Discover the lands of Kainos
World Map — Explore the interactive world map
Jasper Lee Wilkins — Meet the characters

locations/Deros/Agathon.title: Agathon
locations/Deros/Agathon.content:
Agathon
Description

A modest town nestled in a shallow valley, built around an old stone cathedral that has stood since before anyone can remember. The cathedral’s bell tower rises above the surrounding buildings — once a beacon for pilgrims, now just the tallest thing for miles. The streets are cobbled near the center and turn to packed dirt at the edges. It has the feel of a place that was once more important than it is now — buildings slightly too grand for their current use, a market square slightly too large for its current traffic.

What’s Known
Agathon grew around its cathedral centuries ago, when the site drew pilgrims from across Deros. The faith that founded it has faded in prominence, but the town endured as a regional hub — larger than Tethermoor, with a proper market, a mayor, and enough trade to sustain itself comfortably.
The town sits south of Tethermoor, deeper into the south-eastern interior of Deros. Travelers between the two make the journey in roughly four to five days on foot.
It’s well known that Agathon has fallen to The Arkanis. The occupation is open and undisguised — the town’s cathedral repurposed, its mayor replaced. Merchants have stopped passing through, and refugees who made it north and west carry stories of soldiers in the streets and public punishments in the square.

locations/Deros/Astran.title: Astran
locations/Deros/Astran.content:
Astran

Description

The largest city on the continent — a proud, ancient capital that sprawls along both banks of a wide river, its skyline dominated by the Citadel at its center where the Keystone pulses with faint light. Grand stone bridges arch over the water, connecting districts that range from gleaming arcane towers and noble estates to densely packed lower wards where the streets are narrow and the magic is practical rather than pretty. Everything here feels layered — old stone beneath new construction, ancient traditions beneath modern ambition.

The Resonance
Astran is governed by a system unlike any other — the Resonance. At the heart of the Citadel sits the Keystone, a massive crystalline structure of unknown origin that predates the city itself. The Keystone absorbs the collective emotional energy of Astran’s citizens and produces shifting harmonic tones and light patterns.
Each year, a new group of Listeners is selected — citizens who demonstrate an innate ability to sense the Keystone’s patterns. The selection process is public and rigorous: aspirants stand with their backs to the Keystone while it displays sigils to a citizen audience. Those who can accurately repeat what the crystal shows without observing it are deemed attuned and serve as Listeners for the year.
The Listeners interpret the Keystone’s resonance into policy — taxation, defense, trade agreements, justice. It is not a vote and not a decree. It is, the people of Astran largely believe, the collective will of the city made manifest.
Notable Features

The Citadel — the ancient fortress at the city’s center, housing the Keystone and the Listener chambers
The Noble Quarter — home to Astran’s wealthiest families, with arcane streetlights, warded estates, and shops selling enchanted goods
The River Wards — the working heart of the city, sprawling along the riverbanks with markets, docks, warehouses, and taverns
The Shantytowns — the city’s poorest districts, pressed against the outer walls, where magic is scarce and life is hard
TBD 3-4 more places

What’s Known
Astran is the capital of Restrend and the most populous city on Deros. It has stood for centuries, growing around the Keystone and the Citadel that houses it. The city is a melting pot — every species, trade, and tradition on the continent has a foothold here.

locations/Deros/Drosmere.title: Drosmere
locations/Deros/Drosmere.content:
Drosmere
Description

A small, practical hunting town at the edge of a vast forest. The buildings are timber and fieldstone, built low and sturdy. A central plaza serves as the market and gathering place — trappers selling pelts in the morning, loggers drinking in the evening. The air smells of wood smoke, cured leather, and pine resin. It is a town that works hard, speaks plainly, and does not go into the forest after dark.

Life on the Edge
Drosmere’s economy runs on what comes out of Elderfen Forest — timber, game, pelts, and foraged herbs. The people here are hunters, loggers, and trappers by trade, and they know the forest’s edges well. They’ve logged enough of the western Elderfen to open a proper road through the thinner woods, and most commerce flows along that corridor.
But nobody in Drosmere mistakes familiarity for safety. The deep forest is off-limits by common sense, and stories of things seen and heard beyond the tree line keep even the boldest hunters from straying too far. Whether the fear is justified or simply tradition passed down through generations, it shapes everything about life here.
Notable Features

The Plaza — Drosmere’s central square, where the daily market runs and the town gathers for announcements and disputes
The Timber Yards — logging operations along the Elderfen’s western edge, supplying lumber to Drosmere and nearby settlements
The Hunters’ Lodge — an informal guild hall where trappers and hunters share information, post bounties on dangerous animals, and trade

What’s Known
Drosmere sits in eastern Restrend, a short distance from Elderfen Forest. It’s a small town — too small to matter much politically, but well-known to anyone who trades in timber or pelts from the region. The mayor, Harlan Doss, has held office for as long as most residents can remember and is generally regarded as steady and reliable.
The town’s relationship with the forest is practical but uneasy. They take what they need from its edges and leave the rest alone. Travelers passing through are warned not to wander off the road, and most take the advice.

locations/Deros/Elderfen-Forest.title: Elderfen Forest
locations/Deros/Elderfen-Forest.content:
Elderfen Forest
Description

A vast, ancient forest where the canopy grows so thick that daylight barely reaches the floor. The trees are enormous — gnarled trunks wider than houses, roots that twist above the earth like the bones of something buried long ago. The air is heavy, still, and smells of moss, wet bark, and something older. Sounds carry strangely here. Birdsong stops without warning. Branches creak when there is no wind.

The Road to Drosmere
A well-traveled road cuts through the western edge of the Elderfen, connecting the forest to the nearby town of Drosmere. Along this stretch, the woods are noticeably thinner — generations of logging by the people of Drosmere have opened up the canopy and widened the path into a proper trade road. Sunlight reaches the ground here, and the undergrowth is scrubby rather than wild.
Most travelers stick to this road and don’t stray from it. The deeper forest — where the trees close in and the light fails — is another matter entirely.
Notable Features

The Drosmere Path — the thinned western corridor, well-traveled and relatively safe
Abandoned campsites — scattered through the outer forest, left by hunters or travelers who didn’t stay long

What’s Known
The Elderfen is known by name across eastern Restrend. Hunters and woodcutters from Drosmere work its edges, but few venture deep. The forest has a reputation — people who go too far in don’t always come back, and those who do speak of paths that seem to shift, sounds with no source, and a persistent feeling of being watched.
Whether the danger is real or exaggerated depends on who you ask. The people of Drosmere believe it absolutely.

locations/Deros/Kirkwood.title: Kirkwood
locations/Deros/Kirkwood.content:
Kirkwood

Description

A prosperous timber town nestled against a dense forest that never seems to thin no matter how much wood comes out of it. The main street is paved in sawdust-packed earth, lined with lumber yards, carpentry shops, and the offices of trade factors who buy wood by the cartload. The town walls are — naturally — the finest timber construction in the region, thick and well-maintained. A wide plaza at the center hosts the daily lumber market, where the sound of haggling competes with the distant rhythm of axes from the camps.

The Glade
Kirkwood’s prosperity comes from its forest — locally called the Glade. The trees have amazing renewing properpties, allowing regrowth to happen at faster rates.
The people of Kirkwood attribute this to the Precursors — ancient magic embedded in the land itself. Scholars and arcanists who’ve studied the Glade either confirm it’s got restorative properties, or oddly enough, have never returned.
Kirkwood’s economy is built entirely around this phenomenon. Timber from the Glade is exported across Restrend and beyond, making this modest town disproportionately wealthy for its size.
Notable Features

The Glade — the self-healing forest that fuels the town’s economy
The Lumber Market — the central plaza where timber is bought, sold, and contracted daily
Industry & Factories — due to the large reliable supply, many factories exist here that produce the product instead of just exporting the raw goods.
The Lumber Camps — deep-forest lumber camps where the felling crews work and live during cutting season

What’s Known
Kirkwood sits between Astran and Tethermoor, a short distance inland from the eastern frontier. It is well known across Restrend as the source of the continent’s cheapest and most abundant timber. Merchants, builders, and shipwrights all know the name.
The Glade’s success and rumors of magical renewal are mostly known — the specifics of how or why are not. Most people accept the “precursor blessing” explanation and don’t ask further questions.

locations/Deros/Restrend.title: Restrend
locations/Deros/Restrend.content:
Restrend is a broad and wide nation that borders most of all the other ones in Deros. It has an eastern shoreline that closely borders Lythol. At it’s narrowest point is where Tethermoor sits — perched at the edge of the gap, connected by a never faltering stone bridge called The Span.
It’s capital city is Astran, also the largest city in Deros.  In total, it is home to 3.3M people in over 80 different villages, towns, and cities.

locations/Deros/Tethermoor.title: Tethermoor
locations/Deros/Tethermoor.content:
Tethermoor

Description

Two cliff faces lean toward each other across a dizzying gap, close enough to shout across but too far to jump. Between them hangs a web of rope bridges, chain ferries, and cargo lines — all anchored to a single arch of smooth grey stone that spans the void without support or seam. The air smells of rope tar, cook smoke, and open sky. Wind is constant. Everything creaks.

The Span
An ancient stone arch connecting the two cliffs, predating the village by unknown centuries. No one knows who built it. Serves as the village’s main crossing, meeting point, and informal courthouse — disputes are settled at the center, where neither bank has claim.
What’s Known
Tethermoor began as a waypoint — travelers crossing the Span needed a place to rest on either side. Over generations it grew into a small trade hub. The Span itself is far older than any settlement. The two landmasses have been drifting apart for as long as anyone has kept records.
The village sits on the eastern tip of Deros, straddling the gap between Deros and the continent of Lythol. Technically the village spans two continents and two countries — Restrend on the Deros side and Hoida on the Lythol side — but it is considered part of Deros and Restrend by everyone who lives there.
To the south, Agathon and other settlements dot the interior. Rumors of The Arkanis taking over settlements in the region have reached some travelers, but Tethermoor itself seems untouched — for now.

locations/Deros/index.title: index
locations/Deros/index.content:
Deros
Deros is the continent-fragment the campaign takes place on — one of many landmasses drifting in the fractured world of Kainos. Like all fragments, it was once part of a whole, broken apart during the Fracturing and held in tenuous stability by forces no one fully understands.
Geography
Deros is vast — comparable in scale to any of the great continents, with over a hundred settlements ranging from frontier villages to sprawling city-states.
Nations
Deros is divided into several nations, each with its own governance, culture, and history. Among them:

Restrend — The second-largest nation on the continent, and home to the largest city on the continent, Astran.
Kamber — TBD Culture.
Palago — A mixed-culture nation with forests along its southern tip.
Galinvan  - An elvish nation, generally open and welcoming.
Svendale - A deeply insular elvish nation, xenophobic, mostly forest.
Bru’kal -  A diverse nation home to many non-human species.

(Other nations TBD)
What People Know
Residents of Deros know their continent by name and understand readily that other continents and nations exist beyond the fragments. Travel between fragments is not dangerous, but it is somewhat more expensive than overland travel — requiring airfare of some kind or access to teleportation circles.
The Arkaniss conquest is well understood by Deros’s nations & states. In fact, they are beginning to realize just how badly they underestimated the threat. The south-eastern villages and towns have fallen openly, and the pattern — conquering from the outside in, cutting supply lines, isolating cities — is clear to anyone paying attention. The city-states are moving, unfortunately at glacial speed, to form a resistance against the Arkanis. Whether they will act quickly enough remains to be seen.

locations/Echoridge.title: Echoridge
locations/Echoridge.content:
Echoridge
Description

A set of stone spires rising from a mountainous island, each tower belonging to one of the great houses of the Echoridge Knights. The lower slopes are shrouded in perpetual shadow while the peaks gleam in open sky — towers and keeps built into the rock itself, connected by bridges and carved stairways that wind between the spires. An abyss of darkness pools at its roots. Echoridge is only accessible by flight, making it one of the most isolated and defensible places in the region.

The Dragon Riders
Echoridge is the seat of the Echoridge Knights — an order of dragon riders renowned across the fragments as elite mercenaries, guardians, and occasionally conquerors. The nation’s culture, economy, and identity are built entirely around dragon-riding. The Knights are organized into great houses, each occupying one of the island’s spires.
What’s Known
Echoridge is its own nation and continent, albeit small. It was founded after the Fracturing, when Lazziar Venfiel — known as the Umbraslayer — slew a great wyrm that rose from the shadowlands and built a civilization in the mountains above.
Lazziar is the only monarch Echoridge has ever known. The nation’s capital is Capstone, though the island itself is simply called Echoridge by most.
The Echoridge Knights can be hired by anyone who can reach the Perch — but getting there requires flight, and their services don’t come cheap.

locations/World-Map.title: World Map
locations/World-Map.content:
World Map of Kainos
An interactive map of the known world. Pan, zoom, and click markers to explore locations.

Note: This map is a work in progress. Markers and layers will be added as the campaign unfolds.

lore/Elderfen-Lore-Index.title: Elderfen Lore Index
lore/Elderfen-Lore-Index.content:
Elderfen Lore Index
A reference of people, places, and groups connected to The Elderfen and the weretouched clans.

People

Kael Fenclaw — Longtooth weretouched, Fenclaw clan. Left Greyfall to protect the Elderfen from afar.
Soren Fenclaw — Kael’s mother. Understood his decision to leave.
Aric Fenclaw — Kael’s father. Feels Kael is dishonoring the clans and ancestors.
Ravik Fenclaw — Kael’s brother.
Rokan Wildthane — Kael’s childhood friend.
Theran Ashfang — Female, childhood friend of Kael. Their parents wanted them to marry.
Jasper Lee Wilkins — Human who told Kael about the wider world. “Every road in the world starts somewhere, Kael. Might be time you walked one.”

Places & Locations

Elderfen Forest — Ancient forest home to the weretouched clans. Hidden village of Greyfall within. Druidcraft symbols mark the paths.
Greyfall — Weretouched village in a clearing within the Elderfen. Home to Fenclaw, Greyhide, and Ashfang clans.
Drosmere — Nearby human town. The weretouched protect it from outside threats. Humans there fear the forest.
Tethermoore — Where Kael arrived after leaving Greyfall. Wilkins never told him about it — he found it on his own.

Factions, Clans & Groups

Fenclaw Clan — Longtooth Shifter clan. Border wardens of the Elderfen. Monster hunters. First responders. Emphasize discipline over rage. Proverb: “The forest rewards the wolf that waits.”
Greyhide Clan — Beasthide Shifter clan. The Shields of Greyfall. Village protectors, keepers of law and tradition, trainers of young shifters. Carry the long memory of Greyfall. Saying: “The hide protects what the claw cannot.”
Ashfang Clan — Swiftstride Shifter clan. The Wind of the Elderfen. Druids, healers, scouts, messengers. Most spiritually attuned clan. Keepers of sacred groves. Saying: “The forest speaks fastest to those who run with it.”

Deities

Sylvanus — Nature deity worshipped by Kael.

Concepts & Events

The Fracturing — Major world event. Kael is deeply curious about it — what it is, how it changed the world.
The Second Breath — What Kael calls his shifter transformation.
Weretouched — Shifter people of the Elderfen. Multiple clans with distinct subtypes (longtooth, beasthide, swiftstride).

meta/Getting-Started-with-Kainos.title: Getting Started with Kainos
meta/Getting-Started-with-Kainos.content:
Getting Started with Kainos

Introducing Kainos! Kainos is a fractured world - a planet that physically broken. Some continents are floating entirely on their own. You might find that your country isn’t separated by a large ocean, but the void of space. Clearly magic, unknown and powerful, holds the planet together much like a broken vase.
Because of the fractures, Kainos is considered unstable. The damage to the planet has caused pockets of instability. In some places magic is dying, in others it acts irrational and wild. In some places time flows differently, and others dreams overlap with reality.
Campaign Strategy
Unlike other worlds I have built, players in this campaign are highly encouraged to contribute to the world building. Since the world is mostly a blank state, when you create your character, you can optionally introduce lore. For example, if the village or location your character is from does not exist, create it! Your extra lore can be as simple as a single sentence or as complicated as an entire 7-page lore doc. Either way, you hand it to me (Brandon/DM) and I’ll expand it, ensure it is connected and properly placed, and then run with it!
This type of DnD gameplay is considered “bottom-up” - where we will start with the starting village/city, expand as players explore, as opposed to “top-down” where most of the world and it’s inhabitants are already designed. Your characters will have goals, and it is my job ensure I build multiple paths for you to take to get there as well as put plenty of fun obstacles in the way.
The Practical Stuff

Level 1-12
Every other Saturday at 7pm Pacific
Starts July / August
New players welcome!

Starting Your Character

Level 1
2024 Rules
Point Buy, Fixed (Average) HP. If you want Rolled ABS, that’s fine, just DM me!
Any official WotC content allowed. (species/classes/spells/items/feats/etc)
Any third party content or homebrew needs to be approved.
You start with the gold/equipment your background and class gives you only.
Create a visual description OR find / source / generate / commission art for your character
Create a backstory. It can be as simple as a short paragraph, or as expansive as you’d like. The questionnaire below can help form this if you feel overwhelmed.
Think of a few long term goals for your character, and bring them to session 0.
Read Your Story Hook to learn how your character connects to the campaign’s opening
OPTIONAL: Fill out this New Character Questionnaire

Banned Stuff

Silvery barbs
Boots / Broom of Flying
Helm of Teleportation

Added / Approved Content

meta/New-Character-Questionnaire.title: New Character Questionnaire
meta/New-Character-Questionnaire.content:
New Character Questionnaire
Answer as many or as few as you like. The more you answer, the more your DM has to work with.
Backstory & Origins

How did your character spend their childhood? Where did they grow up, and who were their childhood friends?
What relationship does your character have with their parents and siblings?
When they were a child, what did your character want to be when they grew up?
If your character wasn’t an adventurer, what livelihood would they lead?
How did your character learn the languages that they speak?
What does your character’s name represent to them? (Or: why as a player did you choose this name?)

Appearance & First Impressions

Describe your character’s current appearance: clothes, armor, notable scars and their stories, distinguishing features.
What does your character’s voice or speech sound like?
What would most people think when they first see your character?

Personality & Identity

What are your character’s core moral beliefs?
What would your character say their best trait is?
What is your character’s biggest flaw?
Does your character think more with their heart or their brain?
Does your character care about how they’re perceived by others? How do they change themselves to fit in?
Does your character consider themselves a hero, villain, or something else?
What is your character’s biggest pet peeve?
What is your character’s guiltiest pleasure?

Goals & Motivations

What is your character’s current goal, summed up in one sentence?
What, currently, is your character the most curious about?
Where in the world does your character most want to visit?
Does your character ever want to “settle down” with a spouse, children, house, etc.?
Where does your character see themselves in 20 years?
What does your character want to be remembered by?
If your character was granted a single use of Wish, what would they use it for?

Fears, Regrets & Secrets

What is your character’s greatest fear — deep, irrational?
What is your character’s most recent or frequent nightmare?
What is the biggest mistake your character has ever made, and would they change it if they could?
What memory does your character want to forget the most?
What secret is your character keeping?

Beliefs & Worldview

What deity, if any, does your character worship? What’s their opinion on other people’s worship?
What is your character’s opinion on nobility? On authority?
Does your character have any biases for or against certain species?
What is your character’s relationship with magic? Are they scared of it, wish to know more, indifferent?
What does your character think is the true meaning of life?
What is your character’s opinion on killing others?
How far is your character willing to go for the “greater good”? Do they believe in one at all?
What does your character hope for the afterlife?

Relationships & Loyalty

Has your character ever been in love?
What person does your character admire most?
Who is your character’s biggest rival?
Who does your character owe a debt to — a favor, money, or their life?
If someone your character trusts asked them to act against the party’s best interest, who would they side with?
What decision would the party have to make for your character to consider leaving the group?

Combat & Craft

What is your character’s favorite spell, weapon, combat maneuver, or skill?
Is there any particular weapon, item, or artifact your character longs to find?
What treasure or item collected during the adventure is most important to them?
What stereotypical group role does your character play in a party?

Habits & Tastes

What are your character’s hobbies and interests outside of their class?
If your character had time to pick up any artisan’s tools, game set, or instrument, what would it be?
Does your character have any rituals, habits, or superstitions?
What unusual talents does your character possess?
What is your character’s favorite food? Beverage?
What is most important to your character: health, wealth, or happiness?

The Deep Stuff

What would your character sacrifice everything for?
What is a line your character would never cross?
How does your character feel about keeping secrets from the rest of the party?
How generous is your character? Especially to those they don’t know?
How does your character feel about receiving and giving orders? Are they a leader or a follower?
What makes your character feel safe?
Where does your character feel the most at home?
What is your character’s greatest achievement?
If your character knew they were going to die in a month, how would they spend their remaining time?
How does your character imagine the way they will die?
What type of creature in the world is your character the most intrigued by?
What advice would your character give to a younger version of themselves?
What aspect of your character’s future are they most curious about?

meta/Your-Story-Hook.title: Your Story Hook
meta/Your-Story-Hook.content:
Your Story Hook
Every player character in this campaign knows Jasper Lee Wilkins — a charming, morally-questionable human rogue who’s been a fixture of the adventuring world for decades. He’s the reason you’re all heading to Tethermoor.
The Invitation
You’ve received a letter from Jasper inviting you to his retirement auction in Tethermoor. He says he’s hanging up the knives, selling off his adventuring gear — including some genuinely impressive items he’s collected over the decades — to a select crowd of people who’ve impressed him over the years.
It sounds like vintage Jasper: nostalgic, celebratory, a little too good to be true.
How Do You Know Jasper?
Choose one of the connections below (or roll a d6). This is how your character knows Jasper and why they’d accept his invitation to the auction.

































d6Connection1Former Employer — Jasper hired you for a job once. The pay was short but the experience was real. He still owes you.2Saved Your Life — You were in over your head and Jasper pulled you out. He’s never let you forget it.3Drinking Buddy — You’ve spent more nights than you can count sharing a table (and lies) with Jasper. He’s the most entertaining person you know.4Mutual Job — You worked the same contract. Jasper was… Jasper. Unreliable but undeniably useful. You parted on decent terms.5He Trained You — Jasper taught you something — a fighting trick, a con, a trade skill. He was a terrible teacher but a great example.6You Owe Him — Jasper did you a favor you never repaid. The invitation feels like the bill finally coming due.
What You Know Going In

Jasper has invited you to Tethermoor, a small trade village on the edge of the continent, for a retirement auction of his adventuring gear.
You’ve heard rumors that The Arkanis — a conquering military force — have been taking over villages and towns in the region. But Tethermoor seems far enough out to be safe. Probably.
You may or may not know the other player characters. For many of you, this will be the first time you meet.

What to Prepare

Pick your connection to Jasper from the table above (or make up your own — run it by the DM).
Think about your character’s feelings about Jasper. Do you like him? Trust him? Owe him? Resent him? All of the above?
Have a reason to show up. Maybe it’s loyalty, curiosity, greed (magic items!), or just the fact that you’ve got nothing better to do.
